actions.name: 8. Whitespace: Use appropriate whitespace to separate sectio
actions.description: 8. Whitespace: Use appropriate whitespace to separate sections and improve readability.

actions.name: Follow these guidelines for formatting and structuring your
actions.description: Follow these guidelines for formatting and structuring your markdown content:

actions.name: Code Blocks: Use triple backticks (`) to create fenced code
actions.description: Code Blocks: Use triple backticks (`) to create fenced code blocks. Specify the language after the opening backticks for syntax highlighting (e.g., `c

actions.name: Line Length: Break lines at 80 characters to improve readabi
actions.description: Line Length: Break lines at 80 characters to improve readability. Use soft line breaks for long paragraphs.

rules.description: The following markdown content rules are enforced in the validators:
1. Headings: Use appropriate heading levels (H2, H3, etc.) to structure your content. Do not use an H1 heading, as this will be generated based on the
2. Lists: Use bullet points or numbered lists for lists. Ensure proper indentation and spacing.
4. Links: Use proper markdown syntax for links. Ensure that links are valid and accessible.
6. Tables: Use markdown tables for tabular data. Ensure proper formatting and alignment.
7. Line Length: Limit line length to 400 characters for readability.
9. Front Matter: Include YAML front matter at the beginning of the file with required metadata fields.
Headings: Use `##` for H2 and `###` for H3. Ensure that headings are used in a hierarchical manner. Recommend restructuring if content includes H4, an
Links: Use `[link text](URL)` for links. Ensure that the link text is descriptive and the URL is valid.
Tables: Use `|` to create tables. Ensure that columns are properly aligned and headers are included.

directives.description: 3. Code Blocks: Use fenced code blocks for code snippets. Specify the language for syntax highlighting.
5. Images: Use proper markdown syntax for images. Include alt text for accessibility.
Lists: Use `-` for bullet points and `1.` for numbered lists. Indent nested lists with two spaces.
Images: Use `![alt text](image URL)` for images. Include a brief description of the image in the alt text.
Front Matter: Include the following fields in the YAML front matter:
`microsoft_alias`: The Microsoft alias of the author.
`ai_note`: Indicate if AI was used in the creation of the post.
`summary`: A brief summary of the post. Recommend a summary based on the content when possible.
